brands.name: Wonderly Lights
brands.notes: Outdoor, holiday, and permanent-roofline lighting franchise founded in 2022 by Buzz Franchise Brands (Virginia Beach, VA); shifted from a seasonal-only to a year-round model in 2024 and reported 30% year-over-year systemwide sales growth in 2025. NOTE: legacy DB data attributing this brand to HorsePower Brands is incorrect — Wonderly Lights is a Buzz Franchise Brands company (HorsePower's lighting brand is Blingle), so HorsePower's franchisee-fraud litigation does not attach to this brand.

riskFlags.title: Very new, small system
riskFlags.detail: Founded and franchising only since 2022, with roughly 40 units (39 franchised, 1 company-owned) per the 2025 FDD — limited operating track record for validation.

riskFlags.title: Wide franchisee performance dispersion and seasonality transition
riskFlags.detail: Franchise Times reports 2024 franchisee sales ranged from $15,554 to $417,502 — an extremely wide spread for a young system. The brand only moved from seasonal holiday lighting to a year-round model in 2024, so the year-round economics are largely unproven.

riskFlags.title: Parent company is Buzz Franchise Brands, not HorsePower Brands
riskFlags.detail: Wonderly Lights is owned by Buzz Franchise Brands (portfolio: British Swim School, Pool Scouts, Home Clean Heroes). It has no connection to HorsePower Brands or its franchisee fraud litigation; HorsePower's competing lighting brand is Blingle. Any legacy data linking Wonderly Lights to HorsePower is a misattribution.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / 1851 Franchise deep dive
totalInvestmentMin.note: Year-round model. Franchise Times reports a seasonal-only option at $91,007-$109,832.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / 1851 Franchise deep dive

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory; range per 1851 Franchise
franchiseFee.note: Standard single-territory fee; ranges up to $69,500 for larger multi-territory packages per 1851 Franchise deep dive.

royaltyPercent.note: 7% of gross revenue, plus $200/month technology fee and $30,000 minimum annual local ad spend per 1851 Franchise.

totalUnits.note: Franchise Times (Jan 2026) reports 24 franchise owners operating 42 territories across 15 states.

trainingWeeks.note: Entrepreneur lists 42.5 classroom hours plus 38 on-the-job hours (~2 weeks total).

item19Available.source: Franchise Times (cites 2024 franchisee sales range from disclosure data)
item19Available.note: Disclosure reports a 2024 franchisee sales RANGE ($15,554-$417,502) rather than a system AUV; no reliable average figure published, so avgUnitRevenue is omitted.

isGrowing.note: 30% YoY systemwide sales growth in 2025; signed 3 new franchisees in 2025 and projects 6-10 more in 2026.

brands.name: i4 Search Group
brands.notes: Home-based permanent-placement healthcare recruiting franchise (nursing, advanced practice, allied health, physicians) headquartered in Tampa, FL; founded 2019, franchising since 2021 with roughly 32-39 units. Low investment but an unusually high 14% royalty and no Item 19 financial performance representation in its FDD.

riskFlags.title: High royalty burden (14% plus 3% brand fund)
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 14% of gross sales plus a 3% advertising royalty and ~$234/month technology fee — well above typical franchise royalty levels, materially affecting net margins even in a low-overhead recruiting model.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (corroborated by Vetted Biz and FranchisePayback FDD analyses)

riskFlags.title: Young system with no Item 19 disclosure
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2021 (~32 units per Entrepreneur, 2025) and the FDD does not disclose average unit revenue or any financial performance representation, making earnings validation dependent on direct franchisee calls.

riskFlags.title: No litigation disclosed in FDD Item 3
riskFlags.detail: Third-party FDD analyses report no lawsuits or bankruptcy disclosures in i4 Search Group's FDD Item 3 as of the most recent reviewed filing.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / Vetted Biz (2025 FDD)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Single territory and single specialty line.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / Vetted Biz (2025 FDD)

franchiseFee.note: Standard single-territory/single-specialty fee; ranges up to $80,000 for additional territories/specialty lines.

royaltyPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory / Vetted Biz / FranchisePayback

adFundPercent.note: Entrepreneur lists 3% advertising royalty; FranchisePayback's FDD summary describes it as $750/month + up to 4%.

totalUnits.note: 31 franchised + 1 company-owned per FranchisePayback (2024 FDD); Vetted Biz lists 39 current U.S. locations in its 2025 update.

trainingWeeks.source: i4 Search Group official franchise page / Entrepreneur
trainingWeeks.note: ~90-97 hours of combined virtual and in-person training (Entrepreneur: 53 classroom + 44 on-the-job hours).

item19Available.source: FranchisePayback FDD analysis (no AUV/financial performance representation disclosed)

canRunFromHome.source: i4 Search Group official franchise page ('work from home, 100% portable business')

brands.name: iSmash
brands.notes: Rage-room/experiential entertainment franchise (rage rooms, blacklight splatter painting, axe throwing) founded 2018 in Rochester, NY by Steven Shortino; franchising since late 2021. Has sold 100+ franchise locations but only ~9 were open (8 under construction) as of December 2025 — aggressive territory sales far ahead of operating proof. Franchise development site: ismashfranchise.com.

riskFlags.title: 100+ franchises sold vs. only 9 open locations
riskFlags.detail: The brand's own December 2025 announcement states 100+ franchise locations sold (nearly 90 territories in 18 months) while only 9 locations are open and 8 under construction, with 40 openings projected for 2026. Selling territories far faster than units open is a classic emerging-brand risk: most buyers cannot validate against operating franchisees, and the franchisor's revenue is currently driven heavily by franchise fees rather than royalties.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 based on a tiny, top-weighted sample
riskFlags.detail: The advertised average net profit of $232,285 (27.6% margin, 2026 FDD issued May 8, 2026) is based on the top-performing affiliate-owned location plus franchise locations open longer than one year — a very small sample (the 2025 FDD showed only ~7 total units, 5 franchised and 2 company-owned). Headline profit claims are not representative of a typical new unit.
riskFlags.source: iSmash official franchise investment page; unit counts per FranchisePayback (2025 FDD)

riskFlags.title: Very new franchise system in an unproven niche
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since late 2021 with the first franchise location opened June 2022 (Tampa, FL); the rage-room/experiential entertainment category has little long-term consumer-demand track record. No franchisee litigation or closures found in public sources as of June 2026.

franchiseFee.note: Current fee; the 2025 FDD listed $54,950 per FranchisePayback. Veterans pay $53,955.

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales, no minimums per Franchise Gator listing.

adFundPercent.note: 2% brand fund plus up to an additional 2% per the FDD summary.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Vetted Biz analysis of 2025 FDD Item 19
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average yearly gross sales per the 2025 FDD Item 19 as analyzed by Vetted Biz. CAUTION: derived from a very small sample (system had ~7 units, including company-owned/affiliate locations). The 2026 FDD Item 19 (issued 2026-05-08) separately reports average net profit of $232,285 (~27.6% of gross sales) for the top-performing affiliate location and franchises open >1 year.

totalUnits.note: Open operating locations as of Dec 2025; additionally 8 under construction and 100+ franchise territories sold. Directory listings citing '105 units' count sold territories, not open units.

yearFounded.source: Franchising.com press release (first location opened Oct 2018, Rochester, NY; founder Steven Shortino)

yearFranchising.source: FranchisePayback / Franchise Gator (franchising late 2021; first franchised unit opened June 2022 in Tampa, FL)

fddAvailable.source: iSmash official franchise investment page (2026 FDD issued May 8, 2026); 2025 FDD available via Vetted Biz

item19Available.source: iSmash official franchise investment page (cites Item 19 of 2026 FDD)

multiUnitAvailable.source: iSmash news (7-location multi-unit franchise deal in Greater Philadelphia)

isGrowing.note: Nearly 90 territories sold in 18 months; 40 openings projected for 2026. Note: growth is in territory sales, not yet open units — see riskFlags.
